zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Lord Of Somerset Matthew Prute於1150年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特時，他的父親Sir Vawn Prytherch Prute61歲，母親Marged Verch Cadivor Vawr80歲。他和Not Known Not Known至少有1個兒子。他去世於1210年在他的家鄉享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Lord Of Somerset Matthew Prute于1150年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特时，他的父亲Sir Vawn Prytherch Prute61岁，母亲Marged Verch Cadivor Vawr80岁。他和Not Known Not Known至少有1个儿子。他去世于1210年在他的家乡享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Prute
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Proud  .

variants.name: Proud
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): nickname for a vain or haughtyman, from Middle English prod, prud ‘proud’ (late OldEnglish prūd, from the oblique form of Old Frenchproz).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably a respelling of   Laura  , that coincides in form with the German spelling of the same name and also with the medieval English given name.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Somerset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the English county of Somerset, which takes its name from Somerton in Somerset (see Summerton ) + Old English sǣte ‘settlers, dwellers’. The Somerset family who are Dukes of Beaufort are descended from the Plantagenets. The surname was assumed by Charles Somerset (1460–1520), illegitimate son of Henry Beaufort, Duke of Somerset. The latter was himself descended illegitimately from Edward III through John of Gaunt. A later member of the family was Fitzroy Somerset (1788–1855), who, as Lord Raglan, was the commander of British forces in the Crimea, one of the men responsible for the charge of the Light Brigade.

possibly a habitational name from one or more places called with Old Norse sumar ‘summer’ + sætr ‘shieling, hill pasture’, such as Summerseat in Elton (Lancashire), but decisive northern early bearers are lacking.

variants.name: Summerton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Somerton, particularly in Oxfordshire and Somerset, but also in Lincolnshire and Norfolk, named with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Matthew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Lord Of Somerset Matthew Prute was born in 1150, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Vawn Prytherch Prute, was 61 and his mother, Marged Verch Cadivor Vawr, was 80. He had at least 1 son with Not Known Not Known. He died in 1210, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Not Known Not Known. Il est décédé en 1210, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans.
